73	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}subs.json"
74	  jq -n \
75	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
76	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
77	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
78	    --arg zjq_tag_sprue    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
79	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
80	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
81	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
82	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
83	    --arg zjq_stamp        "${z_stamp}" \
84	    '{
85	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:     $zjq_gar_host,
86	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:     $zjq_gar_path,
87	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:   $zjq_lodes_root,
88	      _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE:    $zjq_tag_sprue,
89	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:    $zjq_tag_vouch,
90	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:  $zjq_trust_grade,
91	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA: $zjq_vouch_schema,
92	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:  $zjq_acquired_by,
93	      _RBGL_LODE_STAMP:   $zjq_stamp
94	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
95	    || buc_die "Failed to compose conclave substitutions blob"
96	
97	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
98	    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Conclave" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" \
99	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}" \
100	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
101	}
102	
103	# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed conclave build and
104	# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
105	# enum). The capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
106	# host-minted stamp in slot_1; the vouch-push step writes no output. Conclave
107	# captures exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package), so exactly one slot is
108	# populated. The provenance envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR
109	# (rbgjl02 pushed it under :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the
110	# touchmark a consumer needs.
111	zrbld_conclave_extract() {

113

115	
116	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}output.json"
117	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
118	
119	  buc_log_args "Conclave output:"
120	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

124	    || buc_die "Failed to read reliquary stamp from conclave output"
125	  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
126	  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Conclave output carried no stamp in slot_1"
127	
128	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
129	    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
130	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY}" \
131	    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
132	  buc_success "Conclave captured Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY})"
133	}
134	
135	######################################################################
136	# External Functions (rbld_*)
137	
138	rbld_conclave() {
139	  zrbld_sentinel
140	
141	  buc_doc_brief "Convene the build-tool cohort into one Lode (reliquary kind, rbi_ld capture)"
142	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
143	
144	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
145	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
146	
147	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
148	  local z_token=""
149	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
150	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
151	
152	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
153	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
154	  # The reliquary kind-letter 'r' matches the legacy inscribe datestamp prefix,
155	  # but the namespaces differ (rbi_ld vs rbi_rq), so no collision.
156	  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
157	
158	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
159	
160	  zrbld_conclave_submit "${z_token}" "${z_stamp}"
161	  zrbld_conclave_extract
162	
163	  buc_success "Conclave complete: build-tool cohort -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
164	}
165	
166	# eof
167

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Lode - reliquary body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
#   conclave — convene the build-tool cohort into a Lode (Director credentials)
# The reliquary rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
# kind-specific data — the conclave recipe (gcrane cohort capture + vouch-push),
# the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact extract — and composes
# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
# step-composition machinery lives here.
#
# Conclave absorbs today's inscribe pull machinery (rbfli_Inscribe + rbgji01),
# retargeted from the rbi_rq/<date>/<tool> sibling-package layout to one rbi_ld
# package holding N member tags (:rbi_<tool>) plus the :rbi_vouch envelope. Both
# steps ride the floating gcrane builder (ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER): the tools captured
# here ARE the reliquary, so capture cannot bootstrap from one — conclave is the
# generation phase the pinning rule permits to run unpinned (RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary).

set -euo pipefail

# Conclave is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
# touchmark to a later explicit yoke-stamp election (the reliquary cutover, a
# separate pace) through two bare single-form chaining facts
# (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND/RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY). The
# provenance envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by
# rbgjl02), never host-side.

######################################################################
# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)

# Internal: compose the conclave capture recipe (gcrane cohort capture + vouch-push)
# and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit
# and poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
# inscribe-borrowed poll ceiling (the cohort copy needs the larger ceiling — same
# work inscribe performs today).
# Args: token stamp
zrbld_conclave_submit() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
  local -r z_stamp="${2:?Stamp required}"

  buc_step "Constructing conclave capture recipe"
  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
  # spine. Both steps ride the floating gcrane builder (busybox entrypoint —
  # gcrane:debug's only shell) — no reliquary bootstrap (conclave IS what captures
  # the reliquary tools; generation-tier, the one phase allowed unpinned).
  local -r z_recipe=(
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl03-conclave-capture.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|conclave-capture|busybox"
    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|assemble-push-vouch|busybox"
  )

  buc_log_args "Composing conclave substitutions blob"
  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}subs.json"
  jq -n \
    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_sprue    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
    --arg zjq_stamp        "${z_stamp}" \
    '{
      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:     $zjq_gar_host,
      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:     $zjq_gar_path,
      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:   $zjq_lodes_root,
      _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE:    $zjq_tag_sprue,
      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:    $zjq_tag_vouch,
      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:  $zjq_trust_grade,
      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA: $zjq_vouch_schema,
      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:  $zjq_acquired_by,
      _RBGL_LODE_STAMP:   $zjq_stamp
    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to compose conclave substitutions blob"

  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Conclave" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" \
    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}" \
    "${z_recipe[@]}"
}

# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed conclave build and
# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
# enum). The capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
# host-minted stamp in slot_1; the vouch-push step writes no output. Conclave
# captures exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package), so exactly one slot is
# populated. The provenance envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR
# (rbgjl02 pushed it under :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the
# touchmark a consumer needs.
zrbld_conclave_extract() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"

  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}output.json"
  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"

  buc_log_args "Conclave output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  local -r z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}stamp.txt"
  jq -r '.slot_1.stamp // empty' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read reliquary stamp from conclave output"
  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Conclave output carried no stamp in slot_1"

  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
  buc_success "Conclave captured Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY})"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbld_*)

rbld_conclave() {
  zrbld_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Convene the build-tool cohort into one Lode (reliquary kind, rbi_ld capture)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
  # The reliquary kind-letter 'r' matches the legacy inscribe datestamp prefix,
  # but the namespaces differ (rbi_ld vs rbi_rq), so no collision.
  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"

  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"

  zrbld_conclave_submit "${z_token}" "${z_stamp}"
  zrbld_conclave_extract

  buc_success "Conclave complete: build-tool cohort -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
}

# eof

content.content:
1	# Memo: Lode podvm capture — cerebro experiment (₢BHAAK)
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-06-08
4	**Heat / pace:** ₣BH (rbk-11-mvp-lode-universal-capture) / ₢BHAAK (lode-podvm-cerebro-experiment)
5	**Venue:** cerebro (Linux x86_64, Ubuntu 24.04, glibc 2.39) — by express, throwaway exception (RBK workstation is bash/curl/openssl/jq only and must stay that way).
6	**Author:** Claude Opus 4.8, driven by Brad.
7	
8	This memo is the durable record of an experiment whose physical evidence (scratch
9	binaries, captured GAR Lodes) is destroyed at teardown by design. It establishes
10	how Recipe Bottle should bring podman-VM machine-os images under control in GAR.
11	It does **not** build the production cloud capture — that is a separate follow-up
12	pace this experiment unblocks.
13	
14	---
15	
16	## 1. Bottom line (the cloud-layer decision)
17	
18	- **Tool: `crane`** for the production cloud capture step. Both crane and oras copy
19	  the podvm artifact with **perfect digest fidelity**; skopeo is confirmed out.
20	  crane wins on: Google's own go-containerregistry (already proven in the retired
21	  RBSOB OCI-layout bridge and across our GCB/GAR), `crane cp`-by-digest is
22	  get-or-error (the loud failure the *recorded* trust grade wants), and it handles
23	  both the OCI-artifact disk leaves **and** ordinary container images uniformly.
24	  **oras is a fully-viable fallback** (artifact-native, also digest-faithful) and
25	  is the better tool if the step ever needs `oras discover`/referrers semantics.
26	- **Auth: `gcrane`, not plain `crane` — ambient Google keychain, zero token
27	  plumbing.** The cloud step uses **`gcrane`** (crane's Google-auth sibling:
28	  identical `cp`/`manifest`/`tag` engine, same go-containerregistry repo) from
29	  `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug` (the `:debug` variant carries a
30	  busybox shell for the bash orchestration). gcrane authenticates via
31	  `google.Keychain`, which matches `*.pkg.dev` (our GAR) and draws credentials
32	  from Application Default Credentials → the GCE metadata server — so on Cloud
33	  Build it auths to GAR ambiently as the Mason SA with **no `crane auth login`, no
34	  in-memory token-fetch, no credential-helper image**. Plain `crane` uses only the
35	  docker-config keychain and would need the explicit-login + token dance; the Cloud
36	  SDK image does not bundle crane at all. This generalizes conclave's existing
37	  ambient model (`gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, no explicit login) from docker to
38	  crane. Full evidence in §9.
39	- **skopeo stays ruled out — but the recorded rationale was half-wrong.** The cinch
40	  ruled it out for *silently skipping foreign/non-distributable layers* (issue 545).
41	  That failure mode **does not apply** to this content: the disk blobs are
42	  `application/zstd`, a **distributable** media type, not `nondistributable`.
43	  skopeo's *actual* disqualifier is that its cooked operations **fatal** on the
44	  empty-config OCI artifact: `unsupported image-specific operation on artifact with
45	  type "application/vnd.oci.empty.v1+json"`. So skopeo fails **loud, not silent** —
46	  still wrong for the job, but for the issue-1608 (strict artifact handling) family,
47	  not the issue-545 (silent skip) family. Update the spec rationale accordingly.
48	- **Capture by *leaf digest*, never by copying the whole tag/index.** The upstream
49	  tag is a multi-arch OCI index that **mixes** real container images and disk
50	  artifacts (native family) and spans 5–15 GB. The step must read the index, select
51	  specific `{disktype × arch}` leaves, and `crane cp <repo>@<leaf-digest>` each one.
52	- **Anti-hollow-mirror guard is a host-side curl, no image tool.** A GAR registry
53	  v2 blob `HEAD` returns `Content-Length`; assert it equals the manifest's declared
54	  layer size. Proven here against 1.13 GB / 245 MB / 200 MB / 194 MB blobs. This is
55	  exactly the cloud-side-curl shape the recorded grade demands.
56	- **Trust grade: `recorded-at-acquisition`**, confirmed appropriate. quay rotates
57	  podvm images out within days and publishes no durable checksum; RB attests the
58	  digest observed at capture (trust-on-first-acquisition).
59	
60	---
61	
62	## 2. Environment & tools (exact versions)
63	
64	| Tool | Version | Acquisition | Linkage |
65	|------|---------|-------------|---------|
66	| crane | **v0.21.6** | `google/go-containerregistry` release, `go-containerregistry_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz` | static ELF |
67	| oras | **v1.3.2** | `oras-project/oras` release, `oras_1.3.2_linux_amd64.tar.gz` | static ELF |
68	| skopeo | **1.22.2** | `quay.io/skopeo/stable@sha256:c7d3c512612f52805023cd38351081dad7e2729fc13d14b701e47c7c8bdd6615` via docker (no official static binary exists) | container |
69	| docker (host) | 29.5.3 | pre-installed on cerebro | — |
70	
71	All three image tools were installed into a single throwaway scratch dir
72	(`~/lode-podvm-scratch/bin`); skopeo as a thin docker-wrapper script. Teardown
73	removes the scratch dir and `docker rmi quay.io/skopeo/stable`.
74	
75	**Credential note:** the experiment used the **Director** identity `canest-dir`
76	(`director-canest-dir@cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001.iam.gserviceaccount.com`). The
77	Director RBRA already resided on cerebro from prior work. A self-contained
78	throwaway token-mint (`mint-token.sh`, SA JWT-bearer flow via openssl+curl) issued
79	the GAR OAuth token, independent of the project kindle — this is also a useful
80	finding (see §6). Post-pace credential hygiene (payor reauth + governor remantle)
81	is the operator's, per the cinch.
82	
83	---
84	
85	## 3. Upstream characterization (the core finding)
86	
87	Anchor version **podman 5.6** (latest version common to both families).
88	
89	### 3.1 Provenance anchors (quay index digests at 2026-06-08)
90	
91	| Family | Tag | Index digest |
92	|--------|-----|--------------|
93	| native `quay.io/podman/machine-os` | `5.6` | `sha256:6aa8bfeb41a8ce76b52277f5a609e5adeb097725ca4c3591c8ce2b4f4446dd47` |
94	| wsl `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl` | `5.6` | `sha256:61bb25d36420d8eedf240cbb12deee90b6ff6e31e2d5aba224f537f8bcafdb05` |
95	
96	(quay rotates podvm; these index digests are valid as observed on 2026-06-08 and
97	may age out within days — the reason the trust grade is *recorded*.)
98	
99	### 3.2 Tag landscape — the families diverge
100	
101	- **native** carries `5.0 … 6.0` + `next`. Versions `5.0–5.3` also publish per-arch
102	  tags (`5.x-amd64`, `5.x-arm64`); **at 5.4 the per-arch tags were dropped** — a
103	  single multi-arch index per version since.
104	- **wsl** carries `5.3–5.6` only. No per-arch tags. **Lags the native family.**
105	- **Consequence:** a capture pipeline must pin **per-family** version + scheme;
106	  it cannot assume version parity or a stable tag convention across families.
107	
108	### 3.3 Manifest structure
109	
110	Both top-level tags are `application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json` (schemaVersion 2,
111	no top-level `artifactType`).
112	
113	- native:5.6 → **10** child manifests, **two heterogeneous axes**:
114	  - 2 **plain** children `{arm64, amd64}`, no disktype → **real container OS images**
115	    (config `oci.image.config.v1+json`, ~65 normal `tar+gzip` layers).
116	  - 8 **disktype** children `{aarch64, x86_64} × {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl}`,
117	    disktype carried in `annotations["disktype"]`, arch in the alt spelling
118	    (`aarch64`/`x86_64`, vs `arm64`/`amd64` on the plain children).
119	- wsl:5.6 → **2** child manifests, `wsl` disktype × `{x86_64, aarch64}`.
120	- The native family's wsl-disktype leaves are **distinct digests** from the
121	  standalone wsl family's — one family does not characterize the other.
122	
123	### 3.4 Leaf shapes
124	
125	| Leaf class | mediaType | config | layers |
126	|---|---|---|---|
127	| **disktype** (qemu/wsl/applehv/hyperv) | `oci.image.manifest.v1+json` | `oci.empty.v1+json` (2 bytes) | **single** `application/zstd` blob; filename in `org.opencontainers.image.title` |
128	| **plain** (amd64/arm64) | oci manifest | `oci.image.config.v1+json` | ~65 `tar+gzip` layers (genuine container image) |
129	
130	- The disk artifacts are **OCI artifacts** (empty-config sentinel + one arbitrary
131	  compressed blob), **not** container images — the modern `oras push`-style shape.
132	- Blobs are `application/zstd` = **distributable**. No foreign/non-distributable
133	  markers anywhere. (This is what invalidates the issue-545 silent-skip concern.)
134	- **Annotation caveat:** the layer `image.title` is unreliable for version/arch —
135	  the 5.6 wsl-family x86_64 leaf is titled `5.0-rootfs-amd64.tar.zst` (stale
136	  version *and* alt arch spelling). **Selection must key on the index descriptor's
137	  `platform` + `annotations.disktype`, never the layer filename.**
138	
139	---
140	
141	## 4. Tool verdict (empirical)
142	
143	| Operation | crane 0.21.6 | oras 1.3.2 | skopeo 1.22.2 |
144	|---|---|---|---|
145	| read index | ✅ `crane manifest` | ✅ `oras manifest fetch` | ✅ `inspect --raw` |
146	| read artifact leaf | ✅ | ✅ (sees descriptor, 474 B) | ✅ raw only |
147	| **cooked inspect of artifact** | n/a | n/a | ❌ **FATAL** empty-config artifact |
148	| **copy artifact → GAR (digest-faithful)** | ✅ (qemu 1.13 GB, exact digest) | ✅ (245 MB, exact digest) | not attempted (ruled out) |
149	
150	`crane cp` and `oras cp` both produced a GAR copy whose manifest digest is
151	**byte-identical** to the upstream leaf digest. crane reported `pushed blob` for the
152	zstd layer and reused the well-known empty-config blob
153	(`sha256:44136fa355b3678a1146ad16f7e8649e94fb4fc21fe77e8310c060f61caaff8a`).
154	
155	---
156	
157	## 5. What was captured (and proven, then banished)
158	
159	Two Lodes in the depot GAR (`rbi_ld`, repo
160	`cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001/cancbhm-canest3bhm100001-gar`, us-central1):
161	
162	**`rbi_ld/vn260608213343`** (kind podvm-native, recorded grade):
163	
164	| member tag | tool | upstream leaf digest | size |
165	|---|---|---|---|
166	| `:rbi_qemu-x86_64` | crane cp | `sha256:a754dbe92b9171538872419734cb6b406eeb15928829f461718bf6e438bd910c` | 1,130,075,172 |
167	| `:rbi_wsl-x86_64` | oras cp | `sha256:8d03454f33c9a21079c4f3d4b1b046abc336c3e2448eb0f3563f1e3faf9f7db7` | 244,634,175 |
168	| `:rbi_vouch` | docker FROM-scratch | (provenance envelope) | — |
169	
170	**`rbi_ld/vw260608213906`** (kind podvm-wsl, recorded grade):
171	
172	| member tag | tool | upstream leaf digest | size |
173	|---|---|---|---|
174	| `:rbi_wsl-x86_64` | crane cp | `sha256:3d6f30202266fc9d6a2e2129bce3702dd489686075f24a4b2694221a8a9ae1d1` | 199,835,738 |
175	| `:rbi_wsl-aarch64` | oras cp | `sha256:0131613d9fdc688eb6688dedc394589fb99bbaa21236d87e78cf1fbf86a0b0dc` | 193,808,928 |
176	| `:rbi_vouch` | docker FROM-scratch | (provenance envelope) | — |
177	
178	Control proofs (all host-side, no image tool):
179	- **divine** (`rbw-ld`) enumerated both as `(cohort: N members)`. divine is
180	  **kind-agnostic for enumeration** — the absent `vn`/`vw` legend lines are
181	  cosmetic (the follow-up pace registers them); the Lodes list correctly anyway.
182	- **anti-hollow-mirror guard**: GAR registry-v2 blob `HEAD` returned HTTP 200 with
183	  `Content-Length` exactly equal to each manifest's declared layer size, for all
184	  four members. No hollow mirror.
185	- **banish** (`rbw-lB`) deleted a Lode (the stray, see §6) in a single
186	  `packages delete`; divine then showed it absent. The two evidence Lodes were
187	  banished at teardown (§7).
188	
189	The `:rbi_vouch` envelope used the existing `rbld-vouch-1` schema
190	(schema/kind/lode/acquired_at/acquired_by/capture_build/trust_grade/signature/
191	members[]), with `trust_grade:"recorded-at-acquisition"` and each member carrying
192	`{name, origin, digest, verification:"recorded", tags[]}`. `members[]` is the
193	cardinality axis (length 2 here), identical in shape to bole's length-1.
194	
195	---
196	
197	## 6. Process notes / spooks
198	
199	- **Credential collapse at mount.** Both Governor (`account not found`) and
200	  Director (`Invalid JWT Signature`) probes failed. Root cause: the Governor SA had
201	  been torn down; the Director key was stale downstream. Repair chain:
202	  `rbw-aM` (payor mantles governor) → `rbw-adr` (roster to confirm identity
203	  `canest-dir`) → `rbw-adI canest-dir` (governor invests director) → re-probe green.
204	  The probe retry loop **mislabels both as "SA propagation race"** even when the
205	  failure is terminal (account-not-found / invalid-signature persist all 10
206	  attempts, 104 s) — a 104 s wait for a condition that will never clear. Worth a
207	  spook: distinguish terminal IAM errors from genuine propagation races and
208	  fail-fast on the former.
209	- **Empty-stamp capture bug (mine).** A `VN_STAMP` read via `$(grep …)` under
210	  `set -u` tripped a harness shell-snapshot quirk (`ZSH_VERSION: unbound variable`)
211	  and returned empty, so an `oras cp` landed in package `rbi_ld/vn` (no stamp)
212	  instead of `rbi_ld/vn260608213343`. Caught immediately by the post-push digest
213	  check; the stray became the banish test subject. Lesson for the cloud step: the
214	  stamp must be computed once and passed as an explicit substitution, never re-read
215	  mid-pipeline.
216	- **Self-contained token mint.** `rbgo_get_token_capture` needs the full project
217	  kindle. For an off-station experiment, a 20-line openssl+curl SA-JWT-bearer mint
218	  reading only the RBRA fields was simpler and is reusable for any
219	  RBRA-on-a-foreign-host situation. Not a production path (production stays
220	  cloud-side), but a handy diagnostic seam.
221	- **skopeo via docker, not static.** No official static skopeo binary exists; the
222	  authoritative throwaway is the `quay.io/skopeo/stable` image. Cleaner than a
223	  third-party static rebuild of unknown provenance.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## 7. The follow-up cloud pace (what this unblocks)
228	
229	Build the production podvm capture **cloud-side**, riding the spine exactly like
230	underpin/conclave (do NOT touch the workstation to acquire bytes):
231	
232	1. **`rbgjl05-immure-capture.sh`** (new in-pool step, builder =
233	   `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`, ambient GAR auth via the Google
234	   keychain — see §9; entrypoint the busybox shell). It must:
235	   - read the family index at the nameplate-pinned podman version,
236	   - select the curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set from the index **by descriptor
237	     platform+disktype** (not by layer filename),
238	   - `crane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> <GAR>/rbi_ld/<vn|vw><stamp>:rbi_<variant>`
239	     per selected leaf (by-digest, loud-fail),
240	   - run the **blob-residency guard** (registry-v2 `HEAD`, Content-Length ==
241	     declared size) before vouching,
242	   - then hand off to the existing **`rbgjl02`** vouch step unchanged.
243	2. **`rbldv_*.sh`** body — podvm is **opaque-blob × multi-member**, a blend of
244	   `rbldw_Underpin` (opaque-blob) and `rbldr_Reliquary` (multi-member cohort).
245	   It composes a spine recipe (capture step + vouch step) plus a substitutions blob
246	   carrying family, version, and the declarative selected-platform list. **One verb
247	   `immure` (`rbw-lI`)** spans both quay families via a family/archive argument
248	   (per the paddock Vocabulary), not two verbs.
249	3. **Registration surface** (the 12f48cbab spook — don't miss the single-fixture
250	   registry): `rbgc_Constants.sh` brands/tags (kind-letters `vn`/`vw` already
251	   reserved), `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` divine legend (add vn/vw lines), and the theurge
252	   fixture registry across `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` + `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` **including
253	   `RBTDRC_FIXTURES`**.
254	4. **Theurge fixture** `podvm-lifecycle` (service tier): immure → divine
255	   (enumerate + cohort count) → inspect members + `:rbi_vouch` → per-member +
256	   whole-Lode banish → absent. Add to service + complete suites.
257	5. **Declarative intent (no FQIN).** The nameplate/host-tier config declares family
258	   + version + desired `{disktype, arch}` set; `rbgjl05` resolves leaf digests from
259	   the live index at capture time. Support a "widen selected set against the same
260	   upstream version" refresh mode, separate from "bump podman version" — quay's
261	   hostile retention can make later expansion impossible once a version ages out.
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 8. Premises confirmed / nuanced
266	
267	- **Cloud-side acquisition premise STANDS.** cerebro was a one-time lab bench; the
268	  experiment did not tempt a workstation-hosted production path.
269	- **podvm-selective retention STANDS** — captured 2 curated leaves per family, not
270	  the 5–15 GB full index.
271	- **recorded grade STANDS** and is correctly the *only* honest grade for podvm.
272	- **skopeo-out STANDS, rationale corrected** (artifact-rejection, not silent-skip;
273	  see §1). RBSPV / the Lode spec should be updated to state the real failure mode.
274	- **crane lean CONFIRMED**, with oras documented as an equal-fidelity fallback.
275	
276	---
277	
278	## 9. Cloud-side auth — gcrane ambient Google keychain (2026-06-08, source-read follow-up)
279	
280	Settled after the cerebro teardown by reading go-containerregistry source, not by
281	re-running the bench. Resolves the auth axis the bole-eviction pace cinched as
282	"decided here, inherited downstream" — the experiment used an explicit off-station
283	token mint (§2 credential note, §6 spook) and never characterized the *cloud*
284	auth path. This section is that path.
285	
286	**The binary distinction is load-bearing — `crane` and `gcrane` authenticate differently:**
287	
288	- **`crane`** authenticates only via `authn.DefaultKeychain` (the docker config
289	  file). On a Cloud Build worker that config is empty, so plain crane needs an
290	  explicit `crane auth login` fed by an in-memory metadata-server token — the
291	  skopeo token-fetch dance, ported, not removed.
292	- **`gcrane`** is the Google variant: same `cp`/`manifest`/`tag`/`digest` engine,
293	  but it authenticates through `google.Keychain`. Per `pkg/v1/google/keychain.go`,
294	  that keychain (a) matches `gcr.io`, `*.gcr.io`, **`*.pkg.dev`** (Artifact
295	  Registry — our GAR host), and `*.google.com`, and (b) draws credentials from
296	  Application Default Credentials → the **GCE metadata server** first, gcloud
297	  second, anonymous last. On a Cloud Build worker the Mason SA *is* the
298	  metadata-server identity, so gcrane auths to GAR ambiently with zero auth code.
299	- **The Cloud SDK / gcloud image does not contain crane** — it is gcloud+gsutil+bq
300	  on Debian/Alpine. "A Google image that has gcloud and presumably crane" is a
301	  false premise; that path means installing crane into a fat SDK image yourself.
302	
303	**Decision.** The bole capture step — and every later crane-embrace capture step —
304	uses **`gcrane`** from **`gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`**. The `:debug`
305	variant carries `/busybox/sh` (the bash orchestration) and busybox `sha256sum`
306	(the fingerprint); the non-debug image is distroless — no shell, unusable as a
307	script step. Pin by digest in production, not the floating `:debug` tag. The
308	in-memory `token-fetch` snippet is **dropped** from the bole path; it survives only
309	where a non-keychain tool still needs it, and retires entirely as skopeo and docker
310	are evicted.
311	
312	**This is conclave's model generalized, not a new one.** The reliquary capture step
313	(rbgjl03, RBSLC) already auths ambiently — `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, "no
314	explicit login," cred-helper via the Mason SA. gcrane extends that same ambient
315	posture from docker to crane, so the whole capture family shares one auth story
316	instead of two. The durable canon for this lives in **RBSCB** (Cloud Build
317	posture), which today still enshrines the skopeo "cannot use the credential helper,
318	fetch the token in-memory" rationale that `token-fetch.sh` cites — that line is
319	superseded by this finding and updated as the eviction lands.
320	
321	**Sources:**
322	- go-containerregistry README — crane / gcrane / krane variants and their keychains
323	- `pkg/v1/google/keychain.go` — `isGoogle()` host matching (`*.pkg.dev`) + ADC/metadata credential acquisition
324	- crane image docs — distroless default, `:debug` busybox shell at `/busybox/sh`
325	- Cloud SDK Docker image contents — gcloud/gsutil/bq, no crane
326

# Memo: Lode podvm capture — cerebro experiment (₢BHAAK)

**Date:** 2026-06-08
**Heat / pace:** ₣BH (rbk-11-mvp-lode-universal-capture) / ₢BHAAK (lode-podvm-cerebro-experiment)
**Venue:** cerebro (Linux x86_64, Ubuntu 24.04, glibc 2.39) — by express, throwaway exception (RBK workstation is bash/curl/openssl/jq only and must stay that way).
**Author:** Claude Opus 4.8, driven by Brad.

This memo is the durable record of an experiment whose physical evidence (scratch
binaries, captured GAR Lodes) is destroyed at teardown by design. It establishes
how Recipe Bottle should bring podman-VM machine-os images under control in GAR.
It does **not** build the production cloud capture — that is a separate follow-up
pace this experiment unblocks.

---

## 1. Bottom line (the cloud-layer decision)

- **Tool: `crane`** for the production cloud capture step. Both crane and oras copy
  the podvm artifact with **perfect digest fidelity**; skopeo is confirmed out.
  crane wins on: Google's own go-containerregistry (already proven in the retired
  RBSOB OCI-layout bridge and across our GCB/GAR), `crane cp`-by-digest is
  get-or-error (the loud failure the *recorded* trust grade wants), and it handles
  both the OCI-artifact disk leaves **and** ordinary container images uniformly.
  **oras is a fully-viable fallback** (artifact-native, also digest-faithful) and
  is the better tool if the step ever needs `oras discover`/referrers semantics.
- **Auth: `gcrane`, not plain `crane` — ambient Google keychain, zero token
  plumbing.** The cloud step uses **`gcrane`** (crane's Google-auth sibling:
  identical `cp`/`manifest`/`tag` engine, same go-containerregistry repo) from
  `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug` (the `:debug` variant carries a
  busybox shell for the bash orchestration). gcrane authenticates via
  `google.Keychain`, which matches `*.pkg.dev` (our GAR) and draws credentials
  from Application Default Credentials → the GCE metadata server — so on Cloud
  Build it auths to GAR ambiently as the Mason SA with **no `crane auth login`, no
  in-memory token-fetch, no credential-helper image**. Plain `crane` uses only the
  docker-config keychain and would need the explicit-login + token dance; the Cloud
  SDK image does not bundle crane at all. This generalizes conclave's existing
  ambient model (`gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, no explicit login) from docker to
  crane. Full evidence in §9.
- **skopeo stays ruled out — but the recorded rationale was half-wrong.** The cinch
  ruled it out for *silently skipping foreign/non-distributable layers* (issue 545).
  That failure mode **does not apply** to this content: the disk blobs are
  `application/zstd`, a **distributable** media type, not `nondistributable`.
  skopeo's *actual* disqualifier is that its cooked operations **fatal** on the
  empty-config OCI artifact: `unsupported image-specific operation on artifact with
  type "application/vnd.oci.empty.v1+json"`. So skopeo fails **loud, not silent** —
  still wrong for the job, but for the issue-1608 (strict artifact handling) family,
  not the issue-545 (silent skip) family. Update the spec rationale accordingly.
- **Capture by *leaf digest*, never by copying the whole tag/index.** The upstream
  tag is a multi-arch OCI index that **mixes** real container images and disk
  artifacts (native family) and spans 5–15 GB. The step must read the index, select
  specific `{disktype × arch}` leaves, and `crane cp <repo>@<leaf-digest>` each one.
- **Anti-hollow-mirror guard is a host-side curl, no image tool.** A GAR registry
  v2 blob `HEAD` returns `Content-Length`; assert it equals the manifest's declared
  layer size. Proven here against 1.13 GB / 245 MB / 200 MB / 194 MB blobs. This is
  exactly the cloud-side-curl shape the recorded grade demands.
- **Trust grade: `recorded-at-acquisition`**, confirmed appropriate. quay rotates
  podvm images out within days and publishes no durable checksum; RB attests the
  digest observed at capture (trust-on-first-acquisition).

---

## 2. Environment & tools (exact versions)

| Tool | Version | Acquisition | Linkage |
|------|---------|-------------|---------|
| crane | **v0.21.6** | `google/go-containerregistry` release, `go-containerregistry_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz` | static ELF |
| oras | **v1.3.2** | `oras-project/oras` release, `oras_1.3.2_linux_amd64.tar.gz` | static ELF |
| skopeo | **1.22.2** | `quay.io/skopeo/stable@sha256:c7d3c512612f52805023cd38351081dad7e2729fc13d14b701e47c7c8bdd6615` via docker (no official static binary exists) | container |
| docker (host) | 29.5.3 | pre-installed on cerebro | — |

All three image tools were installed into a single throwaway scratch dir
(`~/lode-podvm-scratch/bin`); skopeo as a thin docker-wrapper script. Teardown
removes the scratch dir and `docker rmi quay.io/skopeo/stable`.

**Credential note:** the experiment used the **Director** identity `canest-dir`
(`director-canest-dir@cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001.iam.gserviceaccount.com`). The
Director RBRA already resided on cerebro from prior work. A self-contained
throwaway token-mint (`mint-token.sh`, SA JWT-bearer flow via openssl+curl) issued
the GAR OAuth token, independent of the project kindle — this is also a useful
finding (see §6). Post-pace credential hygiene (payor reauth + governor remantle)
is the operator's, per the cinch.

---

## 3. Upstream characterization (the core finding)

Anchor version **podman 5.6** (latest version common to both families).

### 3.1 Provenance anchors (quay index digests at 2026-06-08)

| Family | Tag | Index digest |
|--------|-----|--------------|
| native `quay.io/podman/machine-os` | `5.6` | `sha256:6aa8bfeb41a8ce76b52277f5a609e5adeb097725ca4c3591c8ce2b4f4446dd47` |
| wsl `quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl` | `5.6` | `sha256:61bb25d36420d8eedf240cbb12deee90b6ff6e31e2d5aba224f537f8bcafdb05` |

(quay rotates podvm; these index digests are valid as observed on 2026-06-08 and
may age out within days — the reason the trust grade is *recorded*.)

### 3.2 Tag landscape — the families diverge

- **native** carries `5.0 … 6.0` + `next`. Versions `5.0–5.3` also publish per-arch
  tags (`5.x-amd64`, `5.x-arm64`); **at 5.4 the per-arch tags were dropped** — a
  single multi-arch index per version since.
- **wsl** carries `5.3–5.6` only. No per-arch tags. **Lags the native family.**
- **Consequence:** a capture pipeline must pin **per-family** version + scheme;
  it cannot assume version parity or a stable tag convention across families.

### 3.3 Manifest structure

Both top-level tags are `application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json` (schemaVersion 2,
no top-level `artifactType`).

- native:5.6 → **10** child manifests, **two heterogeneous axes**:
  - 2 **plain** children `{arm64, amd64}`, no disktype → **real container OS images**
    (config `oci.image.config.v1+json`, ~65 normal `tar+gzip` layers).
  - 8 **disktype** children `{aarch64, x86_64} × {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl}`,
    disktype carried in `annotations["disktype"]`, arch in the alt spelling
    (`aarch64`/`x86_64`, vs `arm64`/`amd64` on the plain children).
- wsl:5.6 → **2** child manifests, `wsl` disktype × `{x86_64, aarch64}`.
- The native family's wsl-disktype leaves are **distinct digests** from the
  standalone wsl family's — one family does not characterize the other.

### 3.4 Leaf shapes

| Leaf class | mediaType | config | layers |
|---|---|---|---|
| **disktype** (qemu/wsl/applehv/hyperv) | `oci.image.manifest.v1+json` | `oci.empty.v1+json` (2 bytes) | **single** `application/zstd` blob; filename in `org.opencontainers.image.title` |
| **plain** (amd64/arm64) | oci manifest | `oci.image.config.v1+json` | ~65 `tar+gzip` layers (genuine container image) |

- The disk artifacts are **OCI artifacts** (empty-config sentinel + one arbitrary
  compressed blob), **not** container images — the modern `oras push`-style shape.
- Blobs are `application/zstd` = **distributable**. No foreign/non-distributable
  markers anywhere. (This is what invalidates the issue-545 silent-skip concern.)
- **Annotation caveat:** the layer `image.title` is unreliable for version/arch —
  the 5.6 wsl-family x86_64 leaf is titled `5.0-rootfs-amd64.tar.zst` (stale
  version *and* alt arch spelling). **Selection must key on the index descriptor's
  `platform` + `annotations.disktype`, never the layer filename.**

---

## 4. Tool verdict (empirical)

| Operation | crane 0.21.6 | oras 1.3.2 | skopeo 1.22.2 |
|---|---|---|---|
| read index | ✅ `crane manifest` | ✅ `oras manifest fetch` | ✅ `inspect --raw` |
| read artifact leaf | ✅ | ✅ (sees descriptor, 474 B) | ✅ raw only |
| **cooked inspect of artifact** | n/a | n/a | ❌ **FATAL** empty-config artifact |
| **copy artifact → GAR (digest-faithful)** | ✅ (qemu 1.13 GB, exact digest) | ✅ (245 MB, exact digest) | not attempted (ruled out) |

`crane cp` and `oras cp` both produced a GAR copy whose manifest digest is
**byte-identical** to the upstream leaf digest. crane reported `pushed blob` for the
zstd layer and reused the well-known empty-config blob
(`sha256:44136fa355b3678a1146ad16f7e8649e94fb4fc21fe77e8310c060f61caaff8a`).

---

## 5. What was captured (and proven, then banished)

Two Lodes in the depot GAR (`rbi_ld`, repo
`cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001/cancbhm-canest3bhm100001-gar`, us-central1):

**`rbi_ld/vn260608213343`** (kind podvm-native, recorded grade):

| member tag | tool | upstream leaf digest | size |
|---|---|---|---|
| `:rbi_qemu-x86_64` | crane cp | `sha256:a754dbe92b9171538872419734cb6b406eeb15928829f461718bf6e438bd910c` | 1,130,075,172 |
| `:rbi_wsl-x86_64` | oras cp | `sha256:8d03454f33c9a21079c4f3d4b1b046abc336c3e2448eb0f3563f1e3faf9f7db7` | 244,634,175 |
| `:rbi_vouch` | docker FROM-scratch | (provenance envelope) | — |

**`rbi_ld/vw260608213906`** (kind podvm-wsl, recorded grade):

| member tag | tool | upstream leaf digest | size |
|---|---|---|---|
| `:rbi_wsl-x86_64` | crane cp | `sha256:3d6f30202266fc9d6a2e2129bce3702dd489686075f24a4b2694221a8a9ae1d1` | 199,835,738 |
| `:rbi_wsl-aarch64` | oras cp | `sha256:0131613d9fdc688eb6688dedc394589fb99bbaa21236d87e78cf1fbf86a0b0dc` | 193,808,928 |
| `:rbi_vouch` | docker FROM-scratch | (provenance envelope) | — |

Control proofs (all host-side, no image tool):
- **divine** (`rbw-ld`) enumerated both as `(cohort: N members)`. divine is
  **kind-agnostic for enumeration** — the absent `vn`/`vw` legend lines are
  cosmetic (the follow-up pace registers them); the Lodes list correctly anyway.
- **anti-hollow-mirror guard**: GAR registry-v2 blob `HEAD` returned HTTP 200 with
  `Content-Length` exactly equal to each manifest's declared layer size, for all
  four members. No hollow mirror.
- **banish** (`rbw-lB`) deleted a Lode (the stray, see §6) in a single
  `packages delete`; divine then showed it absent. The two evidence Lodes were
  banished at teardown (§7).

The `:rbi_vouch` envelope used the existing `rbld-vouch-1` schema
(schema/kind/lode/acquired_at/acquired_by/capture_build/trust_grade/signature/
members[]), with `trust_grade:"recorded-at-acquisition"` and each member carrying
`{name, origin, digest, verification:"recorded", tags[]}`. `members[]` is the
cardinality axis (length 2 here), identical in shape to bole's length-1.

---

## 6. Process notes / spooks

- **Credential collapse at mount.** Both Governor (`account not found`) and
  Director (`Invalid JWT Signature`) probes failed. Root cause: the Governor SA had
  been torn down; the Director key was stale downstream. Repair chain:
  `rbw-aM` (payor mantles governor) → `rbw-adr` (roster to confirm identity
  `canest-dir`) → `rbw-adI canest-dir` (governor invests director) → re-probe green.
  The probe retry loop **mislabels both as "SA propagation race"** even when the
  failure is terminal (account-not-found / invalid-signature persist all 10
  attempts, 104 s) — a 104 s wait for a condition that will never clear. Worth a
  spook: distinguish terminal IAM errors from genuine propagation races and
  fail-fast on the former.
- **Empty-stamp capture bug (mine).** A `VN_STAMP` read via `$(grep …)` under
  `set -u` tripped a harness shell-snapshot quirk (`ZSH_VERSION: unbound variable`)
  and returned empty, so an `oras cp` landed in package `rbi_ld/vn` (no stamp)
  instead of `rbi_ld/vn260608213343`. Caught immediately by the post-push digest
  check; the stray became the banish test subject. Lesson for the cloud step: the
  stamp must be computed once and passed as an explicit substitution, never re-read
  mid-pipeline.
- **Self-contained token mint.** `rbgo_get_token_capture` needs the full project
  kindle. For an off-station experiment, a 20-line openssl+curl SA-JWT-bearer mint
  reading only the RBRA fields was simpler and is reusable for any
  RBRA-on-a-foreign-host situation. Not a production path (production stays
  cloud-side), but a handy diagnostic seam.
- **skopeo via docker, not static.** No official static skopeo binary exists; the
  authoritative throwaway is the `quay.io/skopeo/stable` image. Cleaner than a
  third-party static rebuild of unknown provenance.

---

## 7. The follow-up cloud pace (what this unblocks)

Build the production podvm capture **cloud-side**, riding the spine exactly like
underpin/conclave (do NOT touch the workstation to acquire bytes):

1. **`rbgjl05-immure-capture.sh`** (new in-pool step, builder =
   `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`, ambient GAR auth via the Google
   keychain — see §9; entrypoint the busybox shell). It must:
   - read the family index at the nameplate-pinned podman version,
   - select the curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set from the index **by descriptor
     platform+disktype** (not by layer filename),
   - `crane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> <GAR>/rbi_ld/<vn|vw><stamp>:rbi_<variant>`
     per selected leaf (by-digest, loud-fail),
   - run the **blob-residency guard** (registry-v2 `HEAD`, Content-Length ==
     declared size) before vouching,
   - then hand off to the existing **`rbgjl02`** vouch step unchanged.
2. **`rbldv_*.sh`** body — podvm is **opaque-blob × multi-member**, a blend of
   `rbldw_Underpin` (opaque-blob) and `rbldr_Reliquary` (multi-member cohort).
   It composes a spine recipe (capture step + vouch step) plus a substitutions blob
   carrying family, version, and the declarative selected-platform list. **One verb
   `immure` (`rbw-lI`)** spans both quay families via a family/archive argument
   (per the paddock Vocabulary), not two verbs.
3. **Registration surface** (the 12f48cbab spook — don't miss the single-fixture
   registry): `rbgc_Constants.sh` brands/tags (kind-letters `vn`/`vw` already
   reserved), `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` divine legend (add vn/vw lines), and the theurge
   fixture registry across `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` + `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` **including
   `RBTDRC_FIXTURES`**.
4. **Theurge fixture** `podvm-lifecycle` (service tier): immure → divine
   (enumerate + cohort count) → inspect members + `:rbi_vouch` → per-member +
   whole-Lode banish → absent. Add to service + complete suites.
5. **Declarative intent (no FQIN).** The nameplate/host-tier config declares family
   + version + desired `{disktype, arch}` set; `rbgjl05` resolves leaf digests from
   the live index at capture time. Support a "widen selected set against the same
   upstream version" refresh mode, separate from "bump podman version" — quay's
   hostile retention can make later expansion impossible once a version ages out.

---

## 8. Premises confirmed / nuanced

- **Cloud-side acquisition premise STANDS.** cerebro was a one-time lab bench; the
  experiment did not tempt a workstation-hosted production path.
- **podvm-selective retention STANDS** — captured 2 curated leaves per family, not
  the 5–15 GB full index.
- **recorded grade STANDS** and is correctly the *only* honest grade for podvm.
- **skopeo-out STANDS, rationale corrected** (artifact-rejection, not silent-skip;
  see §1). RBSPV / the Lode spec should be updated to state the real failure mode.
- **crane lean CONFIRMED**, with oras documented as an equal-fidelity fallback.

---

## 9. Cloud-side auth — gcrane ambient Google keychain (2026-06-08, source-read follow-up)

Settled after the cerebro teardown by reading go-containerregistry source, not by
re-running the bench. Resolves the auth axis the bole-eviction pace cinched as
"decided here, inherited downstream" — the experiment used an explicit off-station
token mint (§2 credential note, §6 spook) and never characterized the *cloud*
auth path. This section is that path.

**The binary distinction is load-bearing — `crane` and `gcrane` authenticate differently:**

- **`crane`** authenticates only via `authn.DefaultKeychain` (the docker config
  file). On a Cloud Build worker that config is empty, so plain crane needs an
  explicit `crane auth login` fed by an in-memory metadata-server token — the
  skopeo token-fetch dance, ported, not removed.
- **`gcrane`** is the Google variant: same `cp`/`manifest`/`tag`/`digest` engine,
  but it authenticates through `google.Keychain`. Per `pkg/v1/google/keychain.go`,
  that keychain (a) matches `gcr.io`, `*.gcr.io`, **`*.pkg.dev`** (Artifact
  Registry — our GAR host), and `*.google.com`, and (b) draws credentials from
  Application Default Credentials → the **GCE metadata server** first, gcloud
  second, anonymous last. On a Cloud Build worker the Mason SA *is* the
  metadata-server identity, so gcrane auths to GAR ambiently with zero auth code.
- **The Cloud SDK / gcloud image does not contain crane** — it is gcloud+gsutil+bq
  on Debian/Alpine. "A Google image that has gcloud and presumably crane" is a
  false premise; that path means installing crane into a fat SDK image yourself.

**Decision.** The bole capture step — and every later crane-embrace capture step —
uses **`gcrane`** from **`gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`**. The `:debug`
variant carries `/busybox/sh` (the bash orchestration) and busybox `sha256sum`
(the fingerprint); the non-debug image is distroless — no shell, unusable as a
script step. Pin by digest in production, not the floating `:debug` tag. The
in-memory `token-fetch` snippet is **dropped** from the bole path; it survives only
where a non-keychain tool still needs it, and retires entirely as skopeo and docker
are evicted.

**This is conclave's model generalized, not a new one.** The reliquary capture step
(rbgjl03, RBSLC) already auths ambiently — `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, "no
explicit login," cred-helper via the Mason SA. gcrane extends that same ambient
posture from docker to crane, so the whole capture family shares one auth story
instead of two. The durable canon for this lives in **RBSCB** (Cloud Build
posture), which today still enshrines the skopeo "cannot use the credential helper,
fetch the token in-memory" rationale that `token-fetch.sh` cites — that line is
superseded by this finding and updated as the eviction lands.

**Sources:**
- go-containerregistry README — crane / gcrane / krane variants and their keychains
- `pkg/v1/google/keychain.go` — `isGoogle()` host matching (`*.pkg.dev`) + ADC/metadata credential acquisition
- crane image docs — distroless default, `:debug` busybox shell at `/busybox/sh`
- Cloud SDK Docker image contents — gcloud/gsutil/bq, no crane

lastPrompt: yes!

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
4	#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
5	#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
6	#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
7	#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
8	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
9	#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
10	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
11	#
12	# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
13	# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
14	# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
15	# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
16	# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
17	# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
18	# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
19	#
20	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
21	# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
22	#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
23	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="
27	
28	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
29	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
30	
31	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
32	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
33	
34	# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
35	# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
36	# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
37	# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
38	# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
39	# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
40	# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
41	# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
42	# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
43	# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.
44	
45	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
46	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
47	
48	# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
49	# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
50	# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
51	MEMBERS=''
52	MFIRST=true
53	
54	while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
55	  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
56	  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
57	  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
58	
59	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"
60	
61	  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
62	  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
63	  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-
64	  # fidelity capture): the reliquary cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on
65	  # amd64 workers, so the other platforms are dead bytes — and a full multi-arch index
66	  # makes the Lode package a parent-index/child-manifest web that GAR's `packages
67	  # delete` cannot unwind in one cascade (it removes a parent, GAR auto-deletes the
68	  # protected children, then the cascade reaches an already-gone child and fails
69	  # NOT_FOUND). Phenomenon + references: RBSLC (conclave) and RBSLB (banish).
70	  gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
71	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }
72	
73	  # Record the upstream manifest-list digest for the envelope. gcrane digest (no
74	  # --platform) streams the tag's stored index digest (sha256:...) — the same canonical
75	  # value docker's RepoDigests reported, unchanged by the single-platform copy above, so
76	  # recorded digests stay identical to the pre-eviction docker path. CBb_101 applies.
77	  DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${UPSTREAM}") \
78	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
79	  test -n "${DIGEST}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
80	
81	  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"
82	
83	  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
84	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
85	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
86	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
87	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
88	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
89	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
90	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
91	done <<'MANIFEST'
92	gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest
93	docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest
94	alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest
95	syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest
96	binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest
97	skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest
98	gcrane|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug
99	MANIFEST
100	
101	# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
102	# the host capture-file). members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
103	# cohort, where bole carries 1.
104	ENVELOPE='{'
105	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
106	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"reliquary\","
107	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
108	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
109	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
110	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
111	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
112	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
113	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"
114	
115	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
116	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
117	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
118	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
119	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
120	
121	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
122	# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
123	RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
124	
125	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
126	echo "${RESULT}"
127	
128	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
129	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
130	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
131	
132	echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
#
# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
#
# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }

PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"

# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — authoritative co-versioned set for
# GCB step execution; mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim (the
# two coexist during the reliquary cutover). gcloud, docker, and gcrane are
# Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane rides the :debug variant
# so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell its capture steps need.
# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
# so the cohort cannot be expressed as the `MANIFEST=( … )` array rbgji01 (running
# under bash) still uses; a while-read over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form,
# matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.

# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
MEMBERS=''
MFIRST=true

while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"

  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"

  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-
  # fidelity capture): the reliquary cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on
  # amd64 workers, so the other platforms are dead bytes — and a full multi-arch index
  # makes the Lode package a parent-index/child-manifest web that GAR's `packages
  # delete` cannot unwind in one cascade (it removes a parent, GAR auto-deletes the
  # protected children, then the cascade reaches an already-gone child and fails
  # NOT_FOUND). Phenomenon + references: RBSLC (conclave) and RBSLB (banish).
  gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Record the upstream manifest-list digest for the envelope. gcrane digest (no
  # --platform) streams the tag's stored index digest (sha256:...) — the same canonical
  # value docker's RepoDigests reported, unchanged by the single-platform copy above, so
  # recorded digests stay identical to the pre-eviction docker path. CBb_101 applies.
  DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${UPSTREAM}") \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
  test -n "${DIGEST}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"

  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
done <<'MANIFEST'
gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest
docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest
alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest
syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest
binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest
skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest
gcrane|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug
MANIFEST

# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
# the host capture-file). members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
# cohort, where bole carries 1.
ENVELOPE='{'
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"reliquary\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"

# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt

# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"

echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 04: Fetch + GPG-verify a WSL rootfs and stage it for the wrap step
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, always pullable; Debian-based
4	#          — carries curl, and gnupg is apt-installed by the gpg-verify-sums snippet
5	#          if absent). The acquisition tool is curl over HTTPS, not a registry pull,
6	#          so this kind needs neither skopeo nor a reliquary bootstrap. This step does
7	#          NOT push: it fetches, verifies, authors the envelope, and stages rootfs.tar
8	#          for the gcrane-append wrap (rbgjl05) — the fetch/verify builder (curl+gpg,
9	#          Debian) and the wrap builder (gcrane:debug busybox) cannot be one image.
10	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
11	#                _RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
12	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY, _RBGL_WSL_URL, _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR
13	#
14	# Fetch the vendor rootfs tarball (URL assembled host-side from the version args)
15	# over HTTPS, DISCOVER its checksum from Canonical's published, GPG-signed
16	# SHA256SUMS (verified against the pinned signing-key fingerprint — the
17	# verified-against-published gate), verify the rootfs bytes against it, then stage the
18	# OPAQUE tarball for the wrap step (rbgjl05), which gcrane-appends it as a single-layer
19	# OCI member (never extracted) into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean
20	# member tag :rbi_rootfs. Author the batch provenance envelope (members[]
21	# length 1 — the cardinality axis) and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch
22	# artifact) and for the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output.
23	# Single-platform (linux/amd64): the rootfs is an opaque blob, not a multi-arch
24	# manifest. No digest is pinned here — the published checksum is discovered and
25	# signature-verified at capture, per the no-FQIN premise (RBSLU).
26	#
27	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
28	# Member tag on that package (clean scheme — no digest/fingerprint layer):
29	#   :<TAG_ROOTFS>   e.g. rbi_rootfs   (the opaque rootfs blob)
30	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
31	#
32	# Trust grade verified-against-published: the member digest recorded in the
33	# envelope is the tarball's GPG-verified published SHA-256, tagged verification
34	# "gpg-sha256-published" with the signing-key fingerprint — NOT an OCI manifest
35	# digest, because what is attested is capture-fidelity of an opaque blob under the
36	# vendor's signed checksum.
37	
38	set -euo pipefail
39	echo "=== Underpin WSL rootfs into a Lode ==="
40	
41	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
42	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
43	
44	URL="${_RBGL_WSL_URL}"
45	KEY_FPR="${_RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR}"
46	test -n "${URL}"     || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_URL missing" >&2; exit 1; }
47	test -n "${KEY_FPR}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR missing" >&2; exit 1; }
48	
49	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
50	MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS}"
51	DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
52	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
53	echo "Source:       ${URL}"
54	
55	# Acquisition moment, attested for the single-member cohort.
56	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
57	
58	# --- Discover the published checksum under the pinned signing key ---
59	# The sums + signature live beside the tarball; the target filename is its
60	# basename. The snippet GPG-verifies the sums and provides EXPECTED_SHA.
61	SUMS_URL="${URL%/*}/SHA256SUMS"
62	SIG_URL="${SUMS_URL}.gpg"
63	TARGET_BASENAME="${URL##*/}"
64	#@rbgjs_include gpg-verify-sums
65	
66	# --- Fetch the rootfs over HTTPS (cloud-side; the workstation never touches these
67	# bytes — only the URL was assembled host-side) ---
68	ROOTFS="/workspace/underpin_${STAMP}.rootfs.tar"
69	echo "--- Fetching rootfs tarball ---"
70	curl -fSL --retry 3 -o "${ROOTFS}" "${URL}" \
71	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch rootfs from ${URL}" >&2; exit 1; }
72	
73	# --- Verify the rootfs bytes against the GPG-verified published checksum ---
74	# Tool availability on the docker builder is a Palisade: sha256sum (coreutils) is
75	# the expected path; shasum / openssl are honest fallbacks so a missing tool fails
76	# the compare loud rather than skipping verification.
77	echo "--- Verifying rootfs against published checksum ---"
78	if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
79	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(sha256sum "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
80	elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
81	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(shasum -a 256 "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
82	elif command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
83	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(openssl dgst -sha256 "${ROOTFS}" | awk '{print $NF}')
84	else
85	  echo "FATAL: no sha256 tool (sha256sum/shasum/openssl) on builder — cannot verify" >&2
86	  exit 1
87	fi
88	test -n "${ACTUAL_SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest computed for rootfs" >&2; exit 1; }
89	
90	if [ "${ACTUAL_SHA}" != "${EXPECTED_SHA}" ]; then
91	  echo "FATAL: rootfs checksum mismatch — refusing to capture unverified bytes" >&2
92	  echo "  published (GPG-verified) sha256: ${EXPECTED_SHA}" >&2
93	  echo "  fetched   sha256:                ${ACTUAL_SHA}" >&2
94	  exit 1
95	fi
96	echo "Verified: sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA} (matches GPG-signed published checksum)"
97	
98	# --- Stage the verified OPAQUE tarball for the wrap step (rbgjl05) ---
99	# The rootfs is staged byte-for-byte into a context dir holding ONLY rootfs.tar;
100	# rbgjl05 gcrane-appends that dir as a FROM-scratch single-layer member (rootfs.tar at
101	# image root, never extracted — gcrane append wraps the blob, it does not unpack it).
102	# The wrap is a separate step because gcrane:debug busybox has no curl/gpg: this Debian
103	# builder fetches and verifies, the gcrane builder wraps and pushes.
104	echo "--- Staging verified rootfs for the wrap step -> ${DEST} ---"
105	CTX="/workspace/underpin_ctx_${STAMP}"
106	mkdir -p "${CTX}"

109	echo "Rootfs verified and staged: ${DEST} (sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA})"

111	# --- Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch
112	# and the host capture-file). members[] length 1 — the wsl singleton. No jq
113	# dependency — values are controlled (member tag, URL, hex digest, key fingerprint,
114	# SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote. ---
115	ENVELOPE='{'
116	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
117	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"wsl\","
118	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
119	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
120	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
121	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
122	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
123	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
124	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
125	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
126	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${URL}\","
127	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA}\","
128	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"gpg-sha256-published\","
129	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signing_key\":\"${KEY_FPR}\","
130	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
131	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
132	
133	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
134	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; underpin produces exactly one Lode.
135	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
136	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
137	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
138	
139	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
140	# underpin produces exactly one Lode.
141	RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
142

144	echo "${RESULT}"
