# Build envelope's `substitutions` field; the spine reads no key from it. The
-# envelope shape (serviceAccount, options.automapSubstitutions, options.logging,
-# options.pool, timeout) is the spine's; the run-as identity is caller-supplied
+# envelope shape (serviceAccount, options.automapSubstitutions, options.substitutionOption,
+# options.logging, options.pool, timeout) is the spine's; the run-as identity is caller-supplied
# (an SA email the spine composes into the depot-project resource path), and the
# environment knobs (TETHER pool, regime timeout) are spine-owned constants.

input.params: {"identity": "BHAAW", "files": ["Tools/rbk/rblds_Spine.sh", "Tools/rbk/rbldl_Lifecycle.sh"], "intent": "Spine: add options.substitutionOption ALLOW_LOOSE so a capture build may MIX a python step (reads its substitutions as automapped env vars, never textual ${_RBGL_*}) with bash steps (textual-ref subs). Live-found: the immure build (python select + bash cp/residency/vouch) failed builds.create with HTTP 400 'key ... not matched in the template' for the 7 select-only substitutions — the bash steps' textual refs flip Cloud Build into strict MUST_MATCH, which then rejects the python-only keys as unreferenced (the all-python delete build escapes this because automap suppresses the check when nothing is textually referenced). ALLOW_LOOSE lifts the strict check; it is safe because the spine's own dispatch-time substitution-coverage scan independently catches the inverse fault (a step ${}-referencing a key the blob omits) that MUST_MATCH would have caught. Doc comments updated (the coverage-check rationale now names ALLOW_LOOSE as its paired relaxation; the envelope-shape list gains options.substitutionOption). rbldl divine legend column widened %-10s -> %-13s so 'podvm-native' (12 chars) no longer overflows into the description. Both verified by a full live lifecycle against the disposable depot: immure podvm-wsl 5.6 -> 2-member Lode vw260610095327 (build SUCCESS 22s, digests byte-identical to upstream), divine cohort + legend, augur envelope decode (recorded grade, honest trust posture), per-member jettison (aarch64 removed, x86_64 + vouch survive), banish (flat-package delete 12s), divine absent."}

content.text: The fixture harness is substantial Rust, well-templated by the existing `wsl-lifecycle` (single-kind capture lifecycle) and `reliquary-lifecycle` (member-jettison) service fixtures. I'll delegate the fixture to a sonnet subagent with the exact behavior I just observed live, and write the specs myself in parallel (disjoint files — Rust vs adoc).

input.description: Write podvm-lifecycle service fixture
input.prompt:
Add a new **service-tier theurge fixture** named `podvm-lifecycle` to the RBK theurge test harness, exercising the just-landed `immure` (podvm) Lode capture verb. Mirror the two existing fixtures that are its templates — do NOT invent a new pattern; copy their exact structure.

## Templates to read and mirror (read these first, in full)
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs` — fixture name constants (`RBTDRM_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE = "wsl-lifecycle"`, `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_RELIQUARY_LIFECYCLE = "reliquary-lifecycle"`, `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_LIFECYCLE`), the `rbtdrm_required_colophons(fixture)` mapping (~line 170 has the lode fixtures), and the `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` registry (which maps fixture name -> tier + case function, and assigns fixtures to suites).
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs` — the actual case functions for `wsl-lifecycle` and `reliquary-lifecycle` (search for where `rbw-lU`, `rbw-lC`, `rbw-lB`, `rbw-la`, `rbw-ld`, `rbw-il`, `rbw-iJ` are invoked). The wsl-lifecycle fn is the closest structural template (single-kind capture -> divine -> augur -> banish, with the touchmark captured from the capture command's stdout). The reliquary-lifecycle fn has the **member-jettison case** (rbw-il + rbw-iJ proving tag-grain delete removes one member and leaves siblings intact) — mirror that case too.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — how suites (service, complete) enumerate fixtures.

Also read `Tools/rbk/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md` for the exact colophon list, and `Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh` + `Tools/rbk/rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` for the verb behavior.

## The lifecycle to encode (VERIFIED LIVE — these are the exact observed behaviors)
The fixture must run this sequence against live GAR and assert at each step. The touchmark is dynamic — capture it from the immure output, exactly as wsl-lifecycle captures the underpin touchmark.

1. **immure**: invoke colophon `rbw-lI` with args `podvm-wsl 5.6` (tabtarget `tt/rbw-lI.DirectorImmuresPodvm.sh podvm-wsl 5.6`). On success it prints `Immure captured Lode <touchmark> — touchmark fact emitted (podvm-wsl)` and `Immure complete: quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.6 -> rbi_ld/<touchmark>`. The touchmark has the form `vw<12 digits>` (kind-letter `vw`). Capture it.
2. **divine** (`rbw-ld`): enumerates Lodes. Assert the captured touchmark appears and is shown as `(cohort: 2 members)`. The kind legend now includes a `vw  podvm-wsl` line.
3. **augur** (`rbw-la <touchmark>`): decodes the `:rbi_vouch` envelope. Assert (STRUCTURE, not exact digest hex — podvm is recorded-at-acquisition grade and the upstream rotates, so NEVER assert the specific sha256 hex): envelope `Kind:` is `podvm-wsl`; `Trust grade:` is `recorded-at-acquisition`; `Members (2)`; both member tags `rbi_wsl-x86_64` and `rbi_wsl-aarch64` are present; member `origin:` lines contain `machine-os-wsl:5.6`; member `verification:` is `recorded`. Also assert the trust-posture honesty text appears (e.g. "attests only the digest observed at capture"). The "Member tags" listing shows exactly 3 tags: `rbi_vouch`, `rbi_wsl-aarch64`, `rbi_wsl-x86_64`.
4. **per-member jettison via the image backdoor** (mirror reliquary-lifecycle's case): `rbw-il rbi_ld/<touchmark>` lists the tags; then `rbw-iJ rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_wsl-aarch64` jettisons ONE member; then `rbw-il rbi_ld/<touchmark>` again must show `rbi_wsl-x86_64` and `rbi_vouch` still present and `rbi_wsl-aarch64` GONE from the TAGS listing (the underlying version lingers untagged — that is expected; assert on the TAGS section only, exactly as reliquary-lifecycle does). If the harness has a confirm-skip mechanism for rbw-iJ (BURE_CONFIRM=skip or similar — check how reliquary-lifecycle drives rbw-iJ non-interactively), use the SAME mechanism.
5. **banish** (`rbw-lB <touchmark>`): cloud-dispatched delete, must end SUCCESS. Use the same non-interactive confirm mechanism the wsl/reliquary lifecycle uses for banish.
6. **divine** (`rbw-ld`) again: assert the touchmark is ABSENT (restored state).

## Registration requirements
- Add `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE: &str = "podvm-lifecycle";` next to the other lode fixture constants, with a one-line comment mirroring the wsl-lifecycle comment (e.g. `// immure -> divine (cohort) -> augur (members + envelope) -> per-member jettison -> banish, restored.`).
- Add its `rbtdrm_required_colophons` arm listing every colophon it drives: `rbw-lI`, `rbw-ld`, `rbw-la`, `rbw-il`, `rbw-iJ`, `rbw-lB` (use the `RBTDGC_*` constants from `rbtdgc_consts.rs` for these — e.g. `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM`; find the exact const names by grepping `rbtdgc_consts.rs`).
- Register it in `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` as a **service**-tier fixture with its case function, and add it to the **service** and **complete** suites (wherever wsl-lifecycle and reliquary-lifecycle are enrolled — match them exactly).
- Write the case function in `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` mirroring the wsl-lifecycle fn's structure (touchmark capture, the cleanup/restore discipline, the assertion helpers it uses like the verdict/expect helpers).

## Constraints
- Do NOT commit. Make the edits only.
- Do NOT hand-edit `rbtdgc_consts.rs` or `claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md` (build-generated).
- The crate must compile: run `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` (NOT raw cargo) and iterate until it builds clean. Do NOT pipe tabtargets through tail/grep (zsh pipefail is off — it masks failures); run the tabtarget, then read `../logs-buk/last.txt`.
- Match the surrounding Rust style (RCG); mirror the existing lifecycle fns' error handling and naming exactly.
- Report: the exact fixture constant name, the case fn name, every file you changed, the build result, and any place where the wsl/reliquary templates diverged from what you needed (so I can review the judgment calls).

When done, report concisely. I will run the live service-tier fixture myself to confirm it passes.

The reliquary-lifecycle fn has the **member-jettison case** (rbw-il + rbw-iJ proving tag-grain delete removes one member and leaves siblings intact) — mirror that case too.
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/main.rs` — how suites (service, complete) enumerate fixtures.

Also read `Tools/rbk/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md` for the exact colophon list, and `Tools/rbk/rbldv_Immure.sh` + `Tools/rbk/rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` for the verb behavior.

## The lifecycle to encode (VERIFIED LIVE — these are the exact observed behaviors)
The fixture must run this sequence against live GAR and assert at each step. The touchmark is dynamic — capture it from the immure output, exactly as wsl-lifecycle captures the underpin touchmark.

1. **immure**: invoke colophon `rbw-lI` with args `podvm-wsl 5.6` (tabtarget `tt/rbw-lI.DirectorImmuresPodvm.sh podvm-wsl 5.6`). On success it prints `Immure captured Lode <touchmark> — touchmark fact emitted (podvm-wsl)` and `Immure complete: quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.6 -> rbi_ld/<touchmark>`. The touchmark has the form `vw<12 digits>` (kind-letter `vw`). Capture it.
2. **divine** (`rbw-ld`): enumerates Lodes. Assert the captured touchmark appears and is shown as `(cohort: 2 members)`. The kind legend now includes a `vw  podvm-wsl` line.
3. **augur** (`rbw-la <touchmark>`): decodes the `:rbi_vouch` envelope. Assert (STRUCTURE, not exact digest hex — podvm is recorded-at-acquisition grade and the upstream rotates, so NEVER assert the specific sha256 hex): envelope `Kind:` is `podvm-wsl`; `Trust grade:` is `recorded-at-acquisition`; `Members (2)`; both member tags `rbi_wsl-x86_64` and `rbi_wsl-aarch64` are present; member `origin:` lines contain `machine-os-wsl:5.6`; member `verification:` is `recorded`. Also assert the trust-posture honesty text appears (e.g. "attests only the digest observed at capture"). The "Member tags" listing shows exactly 3 tags: `rbi_vouch`, `rbi_wsl-aarch64`, `rbi_wsl-x86_64`.
4. **per-member jettison via the image backdoor** (mirror reliquary-lifecycle's case): `rbw-il rbi_ld/<touchmark>` lists the tags; then `rbw-iJ rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_wsl-aarch64` jettisons ONE member; then `rbw-il rbi_ld/<touchmark>` again must show `rbi_wsl-x86_64` and `rbi_vouch` still present and `rbi_wsl-aarch64` GONE from the TAGS listing (the underlying version lingers untagged — that is expected; assert on the TAGS section only, exactly as reliquary-lifecycle does). If the harness has a confirm-skip mechanism for rbw-iJ (BURE_CONFIRM=skip or similar — check how reliquary-lifecycle drives rbw-iJ non-interactively), use the SAME mechanism.
5. **banish** (`rbw-lB <touchmark>`): cloud-dispatched delete, must end SUCCESS. Use the same non-interactive confirm mechanism the wsl/reliquary lifecycle uses for banish.
6. **divine** (`rbw-ld`) again: assert the touchmark is ABSENT (restored state).

## Registration requirements
- Add `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE: &str = "podvm-lifecycle";` next to the other lode fixture constants, with a one-line comment mirroring the wsl-lifecycle comment (e.g. `// immure -> divine (cohort) -> augur (members + envelope) -> per-member jettison -> banish, restored.`).
- Add its `rbtdrm_required_colophons` arm listing every colophon it drives: `rbw-lI`, `rbw-ld`, `rbw-la`, `rbw-il`, `rbw-iJ`, `rbw-lB` (use the `RBTDGC_*` constants from `rbtdgc_consts.rs` for these — e.g. `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM`; find the exact const names by grepping `rbtdgc_consts.rs`).
- Register it in `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` as a **service**-tier fixture with its case function, and add it to the **service** and **complete** suites (wherever wsl-lifecycle and reliquary-lifecycle are enrolled — match them exactly).
- Write the case function in `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` mirroring the wsl-lifecycle fn's structure (touchmark capture, the cleanup/restore discipline, the assertion helpers it uses like the verdict/expect helpers).

## Constraints
- Do NOT commit. Make the edits only.
- Do NOT hand-edit `rbtdgc_consts.rs` or `claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md` (build-generated).
- The crate must compile: run `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` (NOT raw cargo) and iterate until it builds clean. Do NOT pipe tabtargets through tail/grep (zsh pipefail is off — it masks failures); run the tabtarget, then read `../logs-buk/last.txt`.
- Match the surrounding Rust style (RCG); mirror the existing lifecycle fns' error handling and naming exactly.
- Report: the exact fixture constant name, the case fn name, every file you changed, the build result, and any place where the wsl/reliquary templates diverged from what you needed (so I can review the judgment calls).

When done, report concisely. I will run the live service-tier fixture myself to confirm it passes.

[[rbtgo_lode_immure]]
//axvo_method axd_transient axd_grouped
=== {rbtgo_lode_immure}

Captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay family into a single {rbtga_lode}
({rbtgog_lode}),
copying each leaf by digest cloud-side into one `{gar_lodes_namespace}/` package under a host-minted {rbtga_touchmark}.
One verb spans both quay families — `podvm-wsl` (`quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl`) and `podvm-native` (`quay.io/podman/machine-os`) — selected by a family argument, not two verbs.
The {rbtr_director} submits a four-step Cloud Build job — a python select step that reads the upstream OCI index and picks the curated `{disktype × arch}` leaves by descriptor, a gcrane copy-by-digest, a blob-residency guard, then a vouch push — writing the {rbtga_lode_vouch} provenance envelope to its one canonical home, the `:rbi_vouch` tag in GAR, and handing the touchmark forward host-side as the {rbf_fact_lode}.
The podvm kind is opaque-blob (like wsl) × multi-member (like reliquary); intent is declarative (family + version arguments, no FQIN), and the trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition — quay publishes no durable checksum and rotates within days, so RB attests only the digest observed at capture.

include::RBSLI-lode_immure.adoc[]

[[rbtgo_lode_divine]]

content.text: Now the RBSLI subdoc, mirroring RBSLU's structure for the immure operation:

input.content:
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_lode_immure}

NOTE: This operation captures the selected podman-machine disk leaves of one quay
family into a single
{rbtga_lode}
— one
{gar_service}
package under the
{gar_lodes_namespace}
namespace, identified by a host-minted
{rbtga_touchmark}.
It is the podvm kind: a defensive mirror of quay's podman-machine images, which the
upstream rotates within days and never publishes a durable checksum for.
Its payload shape is opaque-blob (like the wsl kind) and its cardinality is
multi-member (like the reliquary kind): each captured leaf is a single-platform OCI
artifact — an empty config plus one `application/zstd` disk blob — riding as one
member tag within the one package.

NOTE: One verb spans both quay families.
`immure` takes the family as its first argument — `podvm-wsl`
(`quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl`, consumed by Windows podman) or `podvm-native`
(`quay.io/podman/machine-os`, consumed by macOS/Linux podman) — and the podman
version as its second (e.g. `5.6`).
The two families diverge in version coverage and platform fan-out, so the kind-letter
(`vw` / `vn`), the upstream family reference, and the curated leaf set are all keyed
on the family argument; there is no kind-argument indirection beyond that one word.

NOTE: Intent is declarative — no FQIN, no pinned digest.
The curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set is an RB constant per family; the
{rbtr_director}
's workstation assembles no coordinate beyond the family and version, and the cloud
step RESOLVES each leaf digest from the live index at capture time.
No upstream digest lives in project config — per the no-FQIN principle, the
declaration stays intent and the pipeline computes the coordinate.

NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a
{gcb_service}
job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
{rbtr_mason}
service account via the metadata server.
The workstation never fetches the multi-gigabyte disk bytes — only the cloud step
copies them, registry-to-registry by digest.

NOTE: The trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition.
quay publishes no durable, re-checkable checksum for podvm and rotates the images
out within days, so RB attests only the leaf digest observed at the moment of
capture — trust-on-first-acquisition, the strongest honest claim the hostile
upstream permits.
The integrity guards are `gcrane cp`-by-digest (get-or-error, digest-faithful) and a
registry-v2 blob-residency check, NOT a re-checkable published checksum.

NOTE: Immure selects leaves by the index DESCRIPTOR, never the layer filename.
The upstream tag is a multi-arch OCI index mixing real container images and disk
artifacts; selection keys on each child descriptor's `platform.architecture` (the alt
spelling `x86_64` / `aarch64`) plus `annotations.disktype`.
The layer's embedded filename is unreliable for version and arch and is never read.

NOTE: Immure bootstraps from no reliquary.
podvm is vessel-less — like wsl — so it has no reliquary slot to resolve tools from;
its index parsing rides python3 on the Google-hosted gcloud builder, and its registry
copy rides gcrane on the floating gcrane builder.
Pinning these floating builders is the bootstrap-builder digest-pin itch, not this
operation.

NOTE: Immure is capture-pure.
It mints the Lode, emits the
{rbtga_lode_vouch}
provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.
Consumption (a host's `podman machine init` from a captured seed, and the nameplate
election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
consumption-side layer that reads the
{rbf_fact_lode}
this operation writes; it is not part of immure.

The
{rbtr_director}

family into a single
{rbtga_lode}

{gar_service}

{gar_lodes_namespace}
namespace, identified by a host-minted
{rbtga_touchmark}.
It is the podvm kind: a defensive mirror of quay's podman-machine images, which the
upstream rotates within days and never publishes a durable checksum for.
Its payload shape is opaque-blob (like the wsl kind) and its cardinality is
multi-member (like the reliquary kind): each captured leaf is a single-platform OCI
artifact — an empty config plus one `application/zstd` disk blob — riding as one
member tag within the one package.

NOTE: One verb spans both quay families.
`immure` takes the family as its first argument — `podvm-wsl`
(`quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl`, consumed by Windows podman) or `podvm-native`
(`quay.io/podman/machine-os`, consumed by macOS/Linux podman) — and the podman
version as its second (e.g. `5.6`).
The two families diverge in version coverage and platform fan-out, so the kind-letter
(`vw` / `vn`), the upstream family reference, and the curated leaf set are all keyed
on the family argument; there is no kind-argument indirection beyond that one word.

NOTE: Intent is declarative — no FQIN, no pinned digest.
The curated `{disktype × arch}` leaf set is an RB constant per family; the
{rbtr_director}
's workstation assembles no coordinate beyond the family and version, and the cloud
step RESOLVES each leaf digest from the live index at capture time.
No upstream digest lives in project config — per the no-FQIN principle, the
declaration stays intent and the pipeline computes the coordinate.

NOTE: Acquisition runs cloud-side.
The
{rbtr_director}
submits a
{gcb_service}
job on the tethered (egress-permitted) pool, executed by the
{rbtr_mason}
service account via the metadata server.
The workstation never fetches the multi-gigabyte disk bytes — only the cloud step
copies them, registry-to-registry by digest.

NOTE: The trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition.
quay publishes no durable, re-checkable checksum for podvm and rotates the images
out within days, so RB attests only the leaf digest observed at the moment of
capture — trust-on-first-acquisition, the strongest honest claim the hostile
upstream permits.
The integrity guards are `gcrane cp`-by-digest (get-or-error, digest-faithful) and a
registry-v2 blob-residency check, NOT a re-checkable published checksum.

The upstream tag is a multi-arch OCI index mixing real container images and disk

spelling `x86_64` / `aarch64`) plus `annotations.disktype`.
The layer's embedded filename is unreliable for version and arch and is never read.

podvm is vessel-less — like wsl — so it has no reliquary slot to resolve tools from;
its index parsing rides python3 on the Google-hosted gcloud builder, and its registry
copy rides gcrane on the floating gcrane builder.
Pinning these floating builders is the bootstrap-builder digest-pin itch, not this
operation.

{rbtga_lode_vouch}
provenance envelope, and reports the touchmark — but writes NO consumer config.

election that pins which touchmark a node provisions) is a separate, deferred
consumption-side layer that reads the
{rbf_fact_lode}
this operation writes; it is not part of immure.

**Done. Here is the concise report.**

## What was done

Four files changed (two source, none generated):

### `/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
- Added `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE: &str = "podvm-lifecycle"` constant after `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE`, with matching one-line comment.
- Added `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE` arm in `rbtdrm_required_colophons` listing all six colophons: `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM`, `RBTDGC_DIVINE_LODES`, `RBTDGC_AUGUR_LODE`, `RBTDGC_LIST_IMAGES`, `RBTDGC_JETTISON_IMAGE`, `RBTDGC_BANISH_LODE`.

### `/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
- Added `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM` to the `use crate::rbtdgc_consts` import block.
- Added five new constants: `RBTDRC_PODVM_FAMILY` (`"podvm-wsl"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_VERSION` (`"5.6"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_TAG_WSL_X86` (`"rbi_wsl-x86_64"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_TAG_WSL_AARCH` (`"rbi_wsl-aarch64"`), `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED` (`"recorded-at-acquisition"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_TRUST_POSTURE_FRAGMENT` (stable substring of the recorded-at-acquisition prose).
- Added `fn rbtdrc_podvm_lifecycle` case function (single case, immure→divine→augur→member-jettison→banish→divine).
- Added `pub static RBTDRC_CASES_PODVM_LIFECYCLE`.
- Added `pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE` (Independent disposition, no setup/teardown).
- Registered in `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` after `RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE`.
- Registered in `service` and `complete` suites after `RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE` (both via `replace_all`).

**Build result:** `Finished dev profile` — clean, zero warnings, zero errors.

---

### Judgment calls vs. templates

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Immure invocation `&[RBTDRC_PODVM_FAMILY, RBTDRC_PODVM_VERSION]` (two args) | `param1` channel: first arg → `BUZ_FOLIO` (family/brand), second → `${1}` (version); mirrors wsl-lifecycle's `&[RBTDRC_WSL_RELEASE, RBTDRC_WSL_POINT]` exactly |
| Trust-grade assert uses `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED` (`"recorded-at-acquisition"`) not `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED` | Podvm upstream offers no durable checksum; spec + code both say recorded grade |
| No digest hex assertion anywhere in augur checks | Spec requirement: "NEVER assert the specific sha256 hex" — upstream rotates |
| Cohort assertion checks `"cohort: 2 members"` in divine output | Literal from `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` jq template: `(cohort: N members)` — structural, not digest-dependent |
| Trust posture fragment checks `"attests only the digest observed at capture"` | Stable substring of `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh`'s fixed prose for RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED branch |
| Jettison removes `rbi_wsl-aarch64`; asserts `rbi_wsl-x86_64` survives | Arbitrary choice of which member to delete, mirroring reliquary-lifecycle's skopeo/gcloud pattern |
| `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED` defined locally (not re-used from existing const) | No existing const for this value in the file; bole/reliquary only needed VERIFIED |</result>

## What was done

Four files changed (two source, none generated):

### `/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
- Added `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE: &str = "podvm-lifecycle"` constant after `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE`, with matching one-line comment.
- Added `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE` arm in `rbtdrm_required_colophons` listing all six colophons: `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM`, `RBTDGC_DIVINE_LODES`, `RBTDGC_AUGUR_LODE`, `RBTDGC_LIST_IMAGES`, `RBTDGC_JETTISON_IMAGE`, `RBTDGC_BANISH_LODE`.

### `/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
- Added `RBTDGC_IMMURE_PODVM` to the `use crate::rbtdgc_consts` import block.
- Added five new constants: `RBTDRC_PODVM_FAMILY` (`"podvm-wsl"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_VERSION` (`"5.6"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_TAG_WSL_X86` (`"rbi_wsl-x86_64"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_TAG_WSL_AARCH` (`"rbi_wsl-aarch64"`), `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED` (`"recorded-at-acquisition"`), `RBTDRC_PODVM_TRUST_POSTURE_FRAGMENT` (stable substring of the recorded-at-acquisition prose).
- Added `fn rbtdrc_podvm_lifecycle` case function (single case, immure→divine→augur→member-jettison→banish→divine).
- Added `pub static RBTDRC_CASES_PODVM_LIFECYCLE`.
- Added `pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURE_PODVM_LIFECYCLE` (Independent disposition, no setup/teardown).
- Registered in `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` after `RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE`.
- Registered in `service` and `complete` suites after `RBTDRC_FIXTURE_WSL_LIFECYCLE` (both via `replace_all`).

**Build result:** `Finished dev profile` — clean, zero warnings, zero errors.

---

### Judgment calls vs. templates

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| Immure invocation `&[RBTDRC_PODVM_FAMILY, RBTDRC_PODVM_VERSION]` (two args) | `param1` channel: first arg → `BUZ_FOLIO` (family/brand), second → `${1}` (version); mirrors wsl-lifecycle's `&[RBTDRC_WSL_RELEASE, RBTDRC_WSL_POINT]` exactly |
| Trust-grade assert uses `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED` (`"recorded-at-acquisition"`) not `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED` | Podvm upstream offers no durable checksum; spec + code both say recorded grade |
| No digest hex assertion anywhere in augur checks | Spec requirement: "NEVER assert the specific sha256 hex" — upstream rotates |
| Cohort assertion checks `"cohort: 2 members"` in divine output | Literal from `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh` jq template: `(cohort: N members)` — structural, not digest-dependent |
| Trust posture fragment checks `"attests only the digest observed at capture"` | Stable substring of `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh`'s fixed prose for RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED branch |
| Jettison removes `rbi_wsl-aarch64`; asserts `rbi_wsl-x86_64` survives | Arbitrary choice of which member to delete, mirroring reliquary-lifecycle's skopeo/gcloud pattern |
| `RBTDRC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED` defined locally (not re-used from existing const) | No existing const for this value in the file; bole/reliquary only needed VERIFIED |</result>

= Podman VM Supply Chain

== Motivation

lines: -= Podman VM Supply Chain
+// Promoted out of FUTURE/ at heat ₣BH. This is an include fragment of RBS0 (the
+// "Podman VM Supply Chain" section); it carries no level-0 title — RBS0 supplies the
+// heading and a +2 leveloffset. The Motivation and Constraints below are the live
+// justification for the podvm Lode kind; the Architecture / Operations / Configuration
+// / Implementation-Status sections describe the SUPERSEDED ignite-VM / GHCR prototype
+// and are retained as historical characterization only — do not resurrect that design.
+[NOTE]
+*Status (heat ₣BH):* the live podvm supply chain is the universal Lode capture model —
+the {rbtgo_lode_immure} operation (RBSLI), `recorded-at-acquisition` trust grade,
+cloud-side gcrane capture into the `{gar_lodes_namespace}/` namespace. The *Motivation*
+and *Constraints* sections below remain the live justification for the podvm kind. The
+*Architecture*, *Operations*, *Configuration*, and *Implementation Status* sections
+describe the *superseded* ignite-VM / GHCR prototype and are kept as historical record
+only; the 2026-06 cerebro section records the experiment that settled the cloud-layer
+choices.
== Motivation

Podman is an excellent open-source tool, but its VM image distribution model creates a serious reproducibility hazard. After months of stable development using a particular Podman version, deleting and redownloading the VM with the same semantic version silently produced a fundamentally different image. The entire SENTRY/BOTTLE network architecture — which had been working reliably — broke. Virtual networking inside containers stopped functioning, and the failure mode gave no indication that the underlying VM image had changed.

file.content:
dropped per-arch tags at 5.4) — pin per-family, never assume parity.

* *Tool verdict.* `crane cp` and `oras cp` both copy a disk leaf to GAR with
  *byte-identical* digest fidelity. skopeo is confirmed out, but on a *different
  ground than first recorded*: its cooked operations *fatal* on the empty-config
  artifact (`unsupported image-specific operation on artifact with type
  "application/vnd.oci.empty.v1+json"`) — a *loud* failure in the strict-artifact
  family, not the *silent* foreign-layer skip the earlier rationale assumed (that
  skip cannot occur here, the blobs being distributable). Production tool choice is
  *crane* (Google's go-containerregistry, get-by-digest-or-error matching the
  recorded trust grade, uniform over artifacts and images), with *oras* an
  equal-fidelity fallback.

* *Cloud direction.* Production capture runs cloud-side on the Lode spine (a new
  `rbgjl05` immure step + `rbldv_` opaque-blob × multi-member body + a
  `podvm-lifecycle` theurge fixture), `crane cp`-ing selected leaves by digest into
  `rbi_ld/<vn|vw><stamp>` and reusing the existing `rbgjl02` vouch step. The
  ignite-VM bootstrap dissolves entirely — Cloud Build carries crane natively. Trust
  grade is `recorded-at-acquisition` (quay publishes no durable checksum and rotates
  within days). The anti-hollow-mirror guard is a host-side GAR registry-v2 blob
  `HEAD` asserting `Content-Length` equals the declared layer size — no image tool.
  See the memo §7 for the follow-up pace's full build sheet.

content.text: Now the cerebro section — convert the production-tool prose crane→gcrane, cite RBSCB, and update the cloud-direction bullet to landed reality. Let me read it.

input.old_string:
skip cannot occur here, the blobs being distributable). Production tool choice is
  *crane* (Google's go-containerregistry, get-by-digest-or-error matching the
  recorded trust grade, uniform over artifacts and images), with *oras* an
  equal-fidelity fallback.

* *Cloud direction.* Production capture runs cloud-side on the Lode spine (a new
  `rbgjl05` immure step + `rbldv_` opaque-blob × multi-member body + a
  `podvm-lifecycle` theurge fixture), `crane cp`-ing selected leaves by digest into
  `rbi_ld/<vn|vw><stamp>` and reusing the existing `rbgjl02` vouch step. The
  ignite-VM bootstrap dissolves entirely — Cloud Build carries crane natively. Trust
  grade is `recorded-at-acquisition` (quay publishes no durable checksum and rotates
  within days). The anti-hollow-mirror guard is a host-side GAR registry-v2 blob
  `HEAD` asserting `Content-Length` equals the declared layer size — no image tool.
  See the memo §7 for the follow-up pace's full build sheet.
input.new_string:
skip cannot occur here, the blobs being distributable). Production tool choice is
  *gcrane* — crane's Google-auth sibling (same `cp`/`manifest`/`digest` engine,
  get-by-digest-or-error matching the recorded trust grade, uniform over artifacts and
  images), authenticating GAR ambiently through its `google.Keychain` so the step needs
  no token plumbing (the ambient-auth canon is RBSCB; the same posture conclave/wsl
  capture already ride). *oras* is the documented equal-fidelity fallback, wired only if
  signed provenance via the OCI referrers API is ever adopted.

* *Cloud direction (LANDED — the {rbtgo_lode_immure} operation, RBSLI).* Production
  capture runs cloud-side on the Lode spine as the `immure` verb (`rbw-lI`, one verb
  spanning both quay families via a family argument), in four steps: a python3 select
  step (`rbgjl07`, on the gcloud builder — it parses the upstream OCI index, which the
  no-jq bash GCB discipline does not cover), a `gcrane cp`-by-digest capture
  (`rbgjl08`), a registry-v2 blob-residency `HEAD` guard asserting `Content-Length`
  equals the declared layer size (`rbgjl09`), and the reused vouch push (`rbgjl02`),
  writing selected leaves into `rbi_ld/<vn|vw><stamp>`. The `rbldv_` body is opaque-blob
  × multi-member, and the `podvm-lifecycle` service fixture proves the lifecycle end to
  end. The ignite-VM bootstrap dissolves entirely — Cloud Build carries gcrane natively.
  Trust grade is `recorded-at-acquisition` (quay publishes no durable checksum and
  rotates within days).

# Step 07 (python3 on the gcloud builder) anon-read the quay family index, selected the
# curated leaves by descriptor platform+disktype, and staged the selection list at
# /workspace/immure_selection.txt (one row: member_tag|leaf_digest|blob_digest|
# blob_size). This step gcrane-cp's each leaf BY DIGEST from the family repo into the
# ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under its member tag, then confirms the copy is
# digest-faithful (cheap manifest readback; the blob Content-Length residency guard
# is rbgjl09). The split exists because parsing the structured upstream index belongs

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJL Step 08: Copy the selected podvm disk leaves into a Lode via gcrane (capture)
# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (floating bootstrap — podvm is
#          vessel-less like wsl, so its tool-pinning defers to the bootstrap-builder
#          digest-pin itch, RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary. gcrane reads the PUBLIC quay
#          source anonymously and auths the GAR push ambiently via google.Keychain
#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh. Auth canon: RBSCB)
# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
#                _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY
#
# Step 07 (Debian builder: curl + jq) anon-read the quay family index, selected the
# curated leaves by descriptor platform+disktype, and staged the selection list at
# /workspace/immure_selection.txt (one row: member_tag|leaf_digest|blob_digest|
# blob_size). This step gcrane-cp's each leaf BY DIGEST from the family repo into the
# ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under its member tag, then confirms the copy is
# digest-faithful (cheap manifest readback; the blob Content-Length residency guard
# is rbgjl09). The split exists because index selection needs jq and the gcrane:debug
# busybox shell carries neither jq nor curl.
#
# gcrane cp <family>@<leaf-digest> is get-or-error (the loud failure the recorded
# trust grade wants) and preserves the manifest digest byte-for-byte (memo-20260608
# §4). Each selected leaf is a single-platform manifest, so the Lode package stays a
# FLAT package (no parent-index web) — banish stays single-call atomic by
# construction (RBSLB). Do not capture the family index itself; that would web the
# package.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Copy selected podvm disk leaves into a Lode (gcrane cp by digest) ==="

STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
FAMILY="${_RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY}"
test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing"   >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${FAMILY}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_PODVM_FAMILY missing" >&2; exit 1; }

# Guard the inter-step handoff (CBi_102): step 07 must have selected the leaves.
test -s /workspace/immure_selection.txt \
  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/immure_selection.txt missing/empty — step 07 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }

PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"

while IFS='|' read -r MEMBER_TAG LEAF_DIGEST BLOB_DIGEST BLOB_SIZE; do
  test -n "${MEMBER_TAG}" || continue
  SRC="${FAMILY}@${LEAF_DIGEST}"
  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
  echo "--- ${MEMBER_TAG}: ${SRC} -> ${DEST} ---"

  # Copy registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push). The
  # leaf is a single-platform OCI artifact (empty config + one zstd blob); gcrane cp
  # copies the manifest and its blob and preserves the manifest digest.
  gcrane cp "${SRC}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${SRC} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Cheap manifest-level integrity check (free on this builder): the copied tag must
  # resolve to the same digest we selected. The deeper blob Content-Length residency
  # guard is rbgjl09 (needs curl — a Debian step). CBb_101 applies.
  DEST_DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${DEST}") \
    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }
  test "${DEST_DIGEST}" = "${LEAF_DIGEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: copied digest mismatch for ${MEMBER_TAG} — ${DEST_DIGEST} != ${LEAF_DIGEST}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "${MEMBER_TAG} captured: ${DEST} (${DEST_DIGEST})"
done < /workspace/immure_selection.txt

echo "=== Immure capture step complete ==="

content.text:
✅ **₢BHAAW (lode-podvm-immure) wrapped** (`3896f9de`).

The podvm `immure` capture vertical is landed and live-proven end-to-end — the last greenfield Lode kind. Across this pace: the four-step cloud pipeline (python select → gcrane cp-by-digest → blob-residency → vouch), the `rbldv` body, divine legend, zipper/tabtarget, RBSLI + the RBS0 quoin, RBSPV promoted out of `FUTURE/`, and the `podvm-lifecycle` service fixture — all green (build · shellcheck 212 · fast 151/0 · the fixture live).

Two things worth carrying forward in your memory of this pace:
- **The mechanism deviated from the docket by design** — the select step is python3, not bash+jq. Your steer plus the architect pass turned a discipline collision (no-jq-in-cloud) into the right shape. The wrap summary records it.
- **The live run earned its keep** — front-loading the capture surfaced the `ALLOW_LOOSE` spine bug that no local check could have caught, and a clean fix dropped in.

The next pace is queued: **₢BHAAL** (lode-podvm-platform-fanout) — the second family + curated multi-platform set + refresh mode I kept deferring. JJK suggests `/clear` then `mount BH` for it, since a fresh context will reorient against the now-landed immure rather than carry this one's scaffolding. Your call when.
